studyGuide.title: Introduction
studyGuide.content: A chemical reaction is a permanent process where ordinary substances mix together and completely change into brand new substances.

studyGuide.title: What is a Chemical Reaction?
studyGuide.content:
It is a change where the original things are lost forever and new materials are formed.
**Example:** Iron + Water + Oxygen = Rust.

studyGuide.title: Types of Reactions
studyGuide.content:
**Combination:** Two things join to make one big thing.
**Decomposition:** One big thing breaks down into small pieces.
**Displacement:** A strong metal pushes a weak metal away.

studyGuide.title: Oxidation & Reduction
studyGuide.content:
**Oxidation** = When a substance gains oxygen (like a freshly cut apple turning brown).
**Reduction** = When a substance loses oxygen.

studyGuide.title: Heat Changes
studyGuide.content:
**Exothermic** = The reaction releases heat and the glass gets hot.
**Endothermic** = The reaction absorbs heat and gets colder, or needs a fire to keep going.

studyGuide.title: Important Signs
studyGuide.content:
How do you know a reaction just happened?
• Changing colors rapidly
• Bubbles or gas shooting out
• The container becomes extremely hot or cold

studyGuide.title: Important Points
studyGuide.content:
• New substances are entirely different from the old ones.
• Mass is never lost! (Law of Conservation of Mass)
• We see this everywhere in daily life: digestion, cooking food, and breathing.
